title: CompTIA Network+ Exam Strategies and Preparation
description: Practice understanding test-taking strategies for the CompTIA Network+ exam including elimination techniques, time management, identifying common traps, reading questions carefully, managing exam anxiety, and maximizing your score.
starterCode:
# EXAM TIPS EXERCISE

## Part 1: Test-Taking Strategies

1. Read each question _______ before looking at the answer choices to understand what is being asked.
2. Look for _______ words like "MOST," "BEST," "FIRST," and "NOT" that change the meaning of a question.
3. The process of _______ involves removing obviously wrong answers to improve your odds of selecting correctly.
4. When two answer choices are very _______, one of them is usually the correct answer.
5. _______ your first instinct — studies show that changing answers usually leads to worse results.
6. For "Choose two" or "Choose three" questions, ensure you select exactly the _______ number specified.
7. Answer every question even if unsure — there is no _______ for wrong answers on the CompTIA exam.

## Part 2: Time Management

8. The Network+ exam allows _______ minutes for up to 90 questions, averaging about one minute per question.
9. Allocate approximately _______ minutes at the start for PBQs, which are more time-consuming.
10. Multiple-choice questions should take an average of 30 to _______ seconds each.
11. If a question is taking too long, _______ it for review and move on to easier questions.
12. Keep track of your _______ throughout the exam using the on-screen timer.
13. Reserve the last _______ minutes to review flagged questions and verify your answers.
14. Complete all _______ questions first since they're quicker, then return to PBQs.

## Part 3: Common Exam Traps

15. _______ answers are choices that are technically correct but don't address what the question specifically asks.
16. Watch for questions that include "_______" — this reverses the question to ask for the WRONG answer.
17. _______ statements in answer choices (always, never, all, none) are usually incorrect.
18. Questions about "the FIRST step" in troubleshooting always start with _______ the problem.
19. CompTIA favors the most _______ approach when multiple solutions could technically work.
20. Be cautious of answers that describe real technologies but are applied in the wrong _______.
21. Vendor-_______ answers that reference specific manufacturer products are typically wrong on CompTIA exams.

## Part 4: Question Analysis Techniques

22. Scenario-based questions contain critical information in the _______ that defines the environment and constraints.
23. Identify the _______ of the question — whether it asks for a device, protocol, command, or concept.
24. Pay attention to network _______ information in scenarios, as it determines which solutions are applicable.
25. When a question mentions specific _______ numbers, it's testing your knowledge of well-known service ports.
26. Questions about troubleshooting typically follow the _______ methodology steps in a specific order.
27. If a question mentions OSI layers, match the answer to the _______ layer being referenced.
28. Questions about "_______ practice" are asking for the industry-standard recommended approach.

## Part 5: Exam Day Preparation

29. Arrive at the testing center at least _______ minutes early to complete check-in procedures.
30. Bring two forms of valid _______ (government-issued photo ID and secondary ID) to the testing center.
31. You will be given a _______ (whiteboard or paper) to use during the exam for notes and calculations.
32. Immediately write down memorized _______ (port numbers, subnet values, mnemonics) on scratch paper.
33. The testing room is monitored by _______ and proctors to prevent cheating.
34. Stay _______ and take deep breaths if you encounter difficult questions — anxiety reduces performance.
35. Do not bring any electronic _______ into the testing room, including phones, watches, and calculators.
36. Review the _______ agreement and exam rules before beginning the actual questions.
37. After completing the exam, you receive your pass/fail _______ immediately on screen.
solution:
# SOLUTION

## Part 1: Test-Taking Strategies

1. Read each question **completely** before looking at the answer choices to understand what is being asked.
2. Look for **keyword** words like "MOST," "BEST," "FIRST," and "NOT" that change the meaning of a question.
3. The process of **elimination** involves removing obviously wrong answers to improve your odds of selecting correctly.
4. When two answer choices are very **similar**, one of them is usually the correct answer.
5. **Trust** your first instinct — studies show that changing answers usually leads to worse results.
6. For "Choose two" or "Choose three" questions, ensure you select exactly the **correct** number specified.
7. Answer every question even if unsure — there is no **penalty** for wrong answers on the CompTIA exam.

## Part 2: Time Management

8. The Network+ exam allows **90** minutes for up to 90 questions, averaging about one minute per question.
9. Allocate approximately **15** minutes at the start for PBQs, which are more time-consuming.
10. Multiple-choice questions should take an average of 30 to **60** seconds each.
11. If a question is taking too long, **flag** it for review and move on to easier questions.
12. Keep track of your **pace** throughout the exam using the on-screen timer.
13. Reserve the last **10** minutes to review flagged questions and verify your answers.
14. Complete all **multiple-choice** questions first since they're quicker, then return to PBQs.

## Part 3: Common Exam Traps

15. **Distractor** answers are choices that are technically correct but don't address what the question specifically asks.
16. Watch for questions that include "**NOT**" — this reverses the question to ask for the WRONG answer.
17. **Absolute** statements in answer choices (always, never, all, none) are usually incorrect.
18. Questions about "the FIRST step" in troubleshooting always start with **identifying** the problem.
19. CompTIA favors the most **cost-effective** approach when multiple solutions could technically work.
20. Be cautious of answers that describe real technologies but are applied in the wrong **context**.
21. Vendor-**specific** answers that reference specific manufacturer products are typically wrong on CompTIA exams.

## Part 4: Question Analysis Techniques

22. Scenario-based questions contain critical information in the **description** that defines the environment and constraints.
23. Identify the **scope** of the question — whether it asks for a device, protocol, command, or concept.
24. Pay attention to network **topology** information in scenarios, as it determines which solutions are applicable.
25. When a question mentions specific **port** numbers, it's testing your knowledge of well-known service ports.
26. Questions about troubleshooting typically follow the **structured** methodology steps in a specific order.
27. If a question mentions OSI layers, match the answer to the **correct** layer being referenced.
28. Questions about "**best** practice" are asking for the industry-standard recommended approach.

## Part 5: Exam Day Preparation

29. Arrive at the testing center at least **15** minutes early to complete check-in procedures.
30. Bring two forms of valid **identification** (government-issued photo ID and secondary ID) to the testing center.
31. You will be given a **scratch** (whiteboard or paper) to use during the exam for notes and calculations.
32. Immediately write down memorized **information** (port numbers, subnet values, mnemonics) on scratch paper.
33. The testing room is monitored by **cameras** and proctors to prevent cheating.
34. Stay **calm** and take deep breaths if you encounter difficult questions — anxiety reduces performance.
35. Do not bring any electronic **devices** into the testing room, including phones, watches, and calculators.
36. Review the **NDA** agreement and exam rules before beginning the actual questions.
37. After completing the exam, you receive your pass/fail **result** immediately on screen.
hints: Read questions completely and look for keywords like MOST, BEST, FIRST, NOT that change the question's meaning.
Process of elimination removes obviously wrong answers; when two choices are very similar, one is usually correct.
No penalty for wrong answers — always answer every question; trust your first instinct on uncertain answers.
Budget ~1 minute per question; flag difficult ones for review; save 10 minutes for final review.
Skip PBQs initially, complete multiple-choice first, then return to PBQs with remaining time.
Distractor answers are technically correct but don't address the specific question being asked.
Absolute statements (always, never, all, none) are usually wrong; CompTIA favors cost-effective solutions.
Vendor-specific answers are typically wrong — CompTIA tests vendor-neutral concepts.
Arrive 15 minutes early with two forms of ID; write memorized information on scratch paper immediately.
Stay calm, manage anxiety with deep breaths; the testing room has cameras and proctors monitoring.
